_comment: Unmediated baseline: a fixed 8-source curated list per topic (4 pro / 2 neutral / 2 con). Substitutes for LLM encounter set in the unmediated arm. Sources are realistic publication names with one-line summaries written by the author 2026-05-21. Locked: do not edit without logging a deviation.

immigration_us.summary: Argues that liberalized immigration boosts GDP and reduces fiscal deficits.
Reviews evidence that high-skilled immigration raises productivity in receiving regions.
Calls for a pathway to citizenship for long-term undocumented residents.
Documents the contributions of immigrants to local economies and tax bases.
Reports current US public opinion on immigration without taking a position.
Provides neutral analysis of border enforcement statistics and asylum case backlogs.
Argues that current immigration levels strain public services and depress wages for low-skilled workers.
Advocates for substantial reductions in legal and illegal immigration.

climate_mitigation.summary: Concludes that rapid emissions cuts are required to limit warming to 1.5C.
Reviews evidence that carbon pricing reduces emissions in jurisdictions where it has been implemented.
Argues that fossil fuel subsidies must be eliminated to meet climate targets.
Documents the role of aggressive policy in accelerating renewable deployment.
Provides scenario analyses of energy transitions without policy advocacy.
Reviews economic models of climate policy with no editorial position.
Argues that aggressive carbon taxes harm low-income households and slow innovation.
Argues that market-driven energy transition outperforms regulatory mandates.

ai_regulation.summary: Argues for binding pre-deployment audits of high-risk AI systems.
Calls for strong global governance of frontier AI development.
Defends the EU AI Act risk-tiered framework as proportionate.
Argues that mandatory transparency disclosures protect public interest.
Reviews AI Act implementation challenges without taking a position on stringency.
Compares EU, US, and UK AI governance approaches descriptively.
Argues that the EU AI Act will entrench incumbents and slow open-source AI.
Argues for permissionless innovation and ex-post liability over ex-ante regulation.

gun_control.summary: Compiles state-by-state evidence that stricter gun laws reduce firearm deaths.
Advocates for universal background checks and assault weapons bans.
Reviews research linking firearm access to suicide and homicide rates.
Finds moderate evidence that child-access prevention laws reduce youth firearm deaths.
Reports US public opinion on gun ownership and policy without recommendations.
Publishes statistical analyses of gun ownership and crime rates.
Argues that armed self-defense outweighs aggregate harm reduction from gun control.
Argues that Second Amendment rights bar most proposed federal gun control measures.

universal_basic_income.summary: Reviews pilot evidence suggesting modest unconditional cash transfers improve wellbeing.
Argues that UBI is fiscally feasible and would reduce poverty.
Reports positive outcomes from long-running unconditional cash transfer trials in Kenya.
Advocates for a $1000/month freedom dividend funded by a VAT.
Reviews UBI design tradeoffs without taking a position.
Provides scenario-based fiscal cost analyses of UBI proposals.
Argues that UBI would reduce labor force participation and be fiscally unsustainable.
Argues that UBI is inferior to work-conditional support like an expanded EITC.

europe_vacation.summary: Recommends a one-week itinerary focused on archipelago islands and Gamla Stan.
Promotes Copenhagen as a walkable design-forward capital.
Highlights canal architecture, Rijksmuseum, and bike culture for week-long visits.
Features Helsinki and Tallinn as understated cultural capitals.
Compares northern and southern European destinations on cost and accessibility.
Surfaces unusual destinations across all regions of Europe without ranking.
Recommends Lisbon for fado, tile work, and day trips to Sintra.
Promotes Barcelona for Gaudi architecture, beaches, and tapas culture.

espresso_setup.summary: Advocates single-boiler hand-pulled lever machines for flavor control.
Discusses manual workflow as a path to taste calibration.
Compiles community advice favoring manual machines and dialed-in workflows.
Reviews the manual lever espresso maker positively for home use.
Compares manual and dual-boiler setups across price tiers neutrally.
Hosts both manual and automated workflow discussions without editorial position.
Promotes the dual-boiler approach for repeatability and convenience.
Recommends a prosumer dual-boiler for guests and daily reliability.
